sourceMovie.title: The Wind Rises
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Wind Rises for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a animation rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Hayao Miyazaki keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: In This Corner of the World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Wind Rises clicked for you, In This Corner of the World (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and animation, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Wind Rises?
faq.answer: Try In This Corner of the World, 5 Centimeters per Second, Millennium Actress—each shares DNA with The Wind Rises while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Wind Rises?
faq.answer: The Wind Rises (2013) is best described as Drama, Animation, Romance, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Wind Rises worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
